rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid and visible in the current scope before using them in assumptions or guarantees.
When AGREE reports type mismatches or '<error>' types, trace the root cause to invalid helper declarations or unresolved identifiers on the same line and correct or remove the problematic clause.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always validate that referenced identifiers and types are properly declared or resolved in the same annex or visible scope.
When generating AGREE contracts, avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers or types that are not present in the base AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
AGREE expressions must have consistent types; use `int`, `real`, or `bool` for scalar declarations and ensure comparisons yield boolean results.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements or references in AGREE contracts that are not already defined in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Avoid generating AGREE clauses that reference identifiers or types that are not visible in the current scope or have unresolved dependencies.
